channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The rise of weather influencers
item.description: “Snow Will Fall Too Fast for Plows,” “ICE STORM APOCALYPSE,” and “Another Big Storm May Be Coming …” were all headlines posted on YouTube this past weekend as the biggest snowstorm in years hit New York City.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Ted Lasso season 4 gets big release window update from Apple
item.description: Apple has tentatively confirmed when Ted Lasso season 4 will be released — and revealed a first look at his new assistant coach.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: Adobe's game-changing Photoshop update finally adds the tool every creative has been asking for – and I can't believe it took this long
item.description: "Control, realism, and precision" - the latest Photoshop updates from Adobe target fast-paced creative professional workflows.
item.dc:creator: Steve Clark, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft launches its second generation AI inference chip, Maia 200
item.description: Signaling that the future of AI may not just be how many tokens an AI model generates, but how optimally it does so, Microsoft has announced Maia 200, which it described as a breakthrough inference accelerator and inference powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI could take down Google’s $260 billion ad empire. Here’s how
item.description: Ads already follow you wherever you go. They’re on your TV, your phone, your train car—even on your airline tray table and escalator. Now, they’ll soon be in your chatbot, too. OpenAI announced last week that it will begin selling ads in ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Smith, Fast Company

item.title: Unplugged holes in the npm and yarn package managers could let attackers bypass defenses against Shai-Hulud
item.description: Javascript developers should consider moving away from the npm and yarn platforms for distributing their work because newly-found holes allow threat actors to run malicious worm attacks like Shai-Hulud, says an Israeli researcher.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why clicking the wrong Copilot link could put your data at risk
item.description: Microsoft Copilot security vulnerability allows attackers to steal data through malicious links. Learn how the attack works, and protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel’s AI pivot could make lower-end PCs scarce in 2026
item.description: In 2026, lower-end PCs may be more difficult to come by, and for those that are available, price tags may rise. This is fallout from Intel’s plans to pivot its manufacturing capacity from chips for PCs to Xeon processors to support intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: National Design Studio looks to overhaul 27,000 federal websites
item.description: The Trump administration has already taken steps to eliminate about 330 websites it deems unnecessary or duplicative. The post National Design Studio looks to overhaul 27,000 federal websites — and is hiring a team to do it first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: GitHub Copilot SDK allows developers to build Copilot agents into apps
item.description: GitHub has launched a technical preview of the GitHub Copilot SDK, a tool kit for embedding the “agentic core” of the GitHub Copilot CLI into applications. Available on GitHub, the SDK was unveiled on January 22. Initially available for Node.js/TypeScript, Python, Go, and .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Go developers meh on AI coding tools – survey
item.description: Most Go language developers are using AI-powered software development tools, but their satisfaction with these tools is middling, according to the 2025 Go Developer Survey. The survey also found that the vast majority of Go developers—91%—were satisfied with using the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reports of SATA’s demise are overblown, but the technology is aging fast
item.description: With the upheaval taking place in the memory market, a new rumor has emerged about the demise of the Serial Advanced Technology Attachment (SATA) drive interface.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rust 1.93 updates bundled musl library to boost networking
item.description: The Rust team has unveiled Rust 1.93, the latest version of the programming language designed to create fast and safe system-level software. This release improves operations involving the DNS resolver for the musl implementation of the C standard library.
item.dc:creator: Network World
